title: genianthus hastatus
intro: Genianthus hastatus, also known as the forest violet or coralroot, is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. It is a climbing plant native to Southeast Asia, and is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant contains alkaloids with anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory infections. Genianthus hastatus can be prepared as a herbal tea, decoction, or tincture, and is also used in traditional folk medicine for its health benefits, including reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing.
